specific_mechanism: PT-LoRA: Parallel-Transport Sequential LoRA. Sequentially fine-tune LoRA on tasks T_1,..,T_k. Constrain B_t ∈ St(r,d). After T_t convergence, parallel-transport B_t from current Stiefel chart to next chart along the geodesic minimizing weight-delta to next task's frame using Edelman-Arias-Smith formula. Continue fine-tuning B_{t+1} on T_{t+1} initialized from transported B_t.
llm_application: PT-LoRA: For sequential fine-tuning on tasks T_1..T_k, constrain LoRA B-matrix to lie on Stiefel St(r,d). When transitioning T_t → T_{t+1}, compute optimal Stiefel geodesic γ from current B_t to a task-T_{t+1}-anchor frame Y_{t+1}. Parallel-transport B_t along γ using the closed-form Edelman-Arias-Smith formula, yielding tilde{B}_t at the new chart. Initialize B_{t+1} = tilde{B}_t and continue Riemannian fine-tuning on T_{t+1}. The intent: preserve T_t adapter geometry as basis rotates between tasks.
novelty_claim: Stiefel-LoRA exists (Riemannian LoRA Stiefel EMNLP 2025 arXiv 2508.17901; StelLA NeurIPS 2025) but PT-LoRA's sequential parallel transport between consecutive tasks as a continual-learning mechanism is more specific.
motivation_justification: Parallel transport is the canonical differential-geometric operation. Stiefel St(p,n) has closed-form parallel-transport formula (Edelman-Arias-Smith). Mechanism transfer is direct.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Stiefel manifold constraint on LoRA B-matrix
M_2: Geodesic from current to next-task anchor frame
